verses.text: They sail on, finding disciples at Tyre. Fellowship everywhere. The church greets its own from town to town.
They sail on, finding disciples at Tyre. Fellowship everywhere. The church greets its own from town to town.
They sail on, finding disciples at Tyre. Fellowship everywhere. The church greets its own from town to town.
The disciples warn Paul through the Spirit not to go up; they kneel and pray on the shore. Loving fear. They dread the danger ahead for him.
The disciples warn Paul through the Spirit not to go up; they kneel and pray on the shore. Loving fear. They dread the danger ahead for him.
The disciples warn Paul through the Spirit not to go up; they kneel and pray on the shore. Loving fear. They dread the danger ahead for him.
At Caesarea they lodge with Philip the evangelist, whose four daughters prophesy. A godly house. The gospel had borne fruit in his family.
At Caesarea they lodge with Philip the evangelist, whose four daughters prophesy. A godly house. The gospel had borne fruit in his family.
At Caesarea they lodge with Philip the evangelist, whose four daughters prophesy. A godly house. The gospel had borne fruit in his family.
Agabus binds himself with Paul's girdle, So shall the Jews bind its owner; they beg him not to go. Warned plainly. The prophecy is clear, yet Paul is unmoved.
Agabus binds himself with Paul's girdle, So shall the Jews bind its owner; they beg him not to go. Warned plainly. The prophecy is clear, yet Paul is unmoved.
Agabus binds himself with Paul's girdle, So shall the Jews bind its owner; they beg him not to go. Warned plainly. The prophecy is clear, yet Paul is unmoved.
'I am ready... to die... for the name of the Lord Jesus'; 'The will of the Lord be done.' Surrendered. Both Paul and they bow to God's will.
'I am ready... to die... for the name of the Lord Jesus'; 'The will of the Lord be done.' Surrendered. Both Paul and they bow to God's will.
They go to Jerusalem, gladly received by the brethren. A warm welcome. The church rejoices to see the apostle.
They go to Jerusalem, gladly received by the brethren. A warm welcome. The church rejoices to see the apostle.
They go to Jerusalem, gladly received by the brethren. A warm welcome. The church rejoices to see the apostle.
James and the elders glorify God for the Gentiles, but tell of many zealous Jews. Tension. The gospel's spread stirs old loyalties.
James and the elders glorify God for the Gentiles, but tell of many zealous Jews. Tension. The gospel's spread stirs old loyalties.
James and the elders glorify God for the Gentiles, but tell of many zealous Jews. Tension. The gospel's spread stirs old loyalties.
Lest they think he forsook Moses, Paul is advised to join four men under a vow. Wise yielding. He bends in things indifferent to avoid offence.
Lest they think he forsook Moses, Paul is advised to join four men under a vow. Wise yielding. He bends in things indifferent to avoid offence.
Lest they think he forsook Moses, Paul is advised to join four men under a vow. Wise yielding. He bends in things indifferent to avoid offence.
Lest they think he forsook Moses, Paul is advised to join four men under a vow. Wise yielding. He bends in things indifferent to avoid offence.
Lest they think he forsook Moses, Paul is advised to join four men under a vow. Wise yielding. He bends in things indifferent to avoid offence.
The Jews of Asia stir the crowd, This is the man that teaches against the law and the temple. False charge. Slander rouses the mob against him.
The Jews of Asia stir the crowd, This is the man that teaches against the law and the temple. False charge. Slander rouses the mob against him.
The Jews of Asia stir the crowd, This is the man that teaches against the law and the temple. False charge. Slander rouses the mob against him.
They drag him out to kill him; the captain rescues him with soldiers. Timely help. God uses Roman order to spare His servant.
They drag him out to kill him; the captain rescues him with soldiers. Timely help. God uses Roman order to spare His servant.
They drag him out to kill him; the captain rescues him with soldiers. Timely help. God uses Roman order to spare His servant.
They drag him out to kill him; the captain rescues him with soldiers. Timely help. God uses Roman order to spare His servant.
Bound with chains, the crowd cries, Away with him; he is borne up the stairs for the violence. Mob fury. He suffers as his Master did.
Bound with chains, the crowd cries, Away with him; he is borne up the stairs for the violence. Mob fury. He suffers as his Master did.
Bound with chains, the crowd cries, Away with him; he is borne up the stairs for the violence. Mob fury. He suffers as his Master did.
Bound with chains, the crowd cries, Away with him; he is borne up the stairs for the violence. Mob fury. He suffers as his Master did.
Paul asks to speak; a Jew of Tarsus, he addresses them in Hebrew. Calm courage. Even arrested, he seeks to win his accusers.
